# dotagent ‚Äî LLM context (single-fetch digest)

> Polyglot agent orchestrator. You write `agent.toml` + a script in any
> language. dotagent schedules, supervises, retries, and notifies on
> failure via OS-native scheduling (launchd / systemd).
>
> This file is a dense, single-page reference optimized for LLMs. After
> reading this you should be able to write a working agent without
> opening any other doc. Links to deep dives are at the bottom.
>
> Source repo: https://github.com/avelino/dotagent
> Site (humans): https://avelino.gitbook.io/dotagent (or the `docs/`
> tree of the repo)
> Spec version: tracks `crates/dotagent-core` ‚Äî when in doubt, the
> Rust source under `dotagent-core/src/manifest.rs` wins.

---

## 1. Mental model in 60 seconds

```
‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê     ‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê     ‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
‚îÇ  launchd/  ‚îÇ ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚ñ∂ ‚îÇ dotagent daemon   ‚îÇ ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚ñ∂ ‚îÇ  your script   ‚îÇ
‚îÇ  systemd   ‚îÇ     ‚îÇ (single process)  ‚îÇ     ‚îÇ  (any language)‚îÇ
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò     ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò     ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò
       OS keeps daemon alive       daemon spawns agents on schedule
```

- The OS scheduler (launchd on macOS / systemd on Linux) keeps the
  daemon alive. The daemon keeps every agent alive.
- One daemon, N agents, M schedules per agent.
- The daemon **never sleeps to wait** ‚Äî it computes the next event and
  uses the OS scheduler.
- Your agent script just reads env vars and exits with a code.
- Failure path: retry ‚Üí exhaust retries ‚Üí fire `[[notifiers]]` (built-in,
  in-process) and/or `[[on_failure]]` plugins (subprocess, JSON stdio).

---

## 2. TL;DR ‚Äî 3 files = 1 working agent

```
~/.config/dotagent/agents/my-agent/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ agent.toml      # manifest (contract)
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ agent.fish      # script (or agent.py / agent.go / a binary)
```

Smallest viable manifest:

```toml
[agent]
name = "my-agent"
timeout_seconds = 300

[run]
command = "python3"
args = ["./agent.py"]

[[schedules]]
id = "every-90"
type = "interval"
interval_minutes = 90

[[notifiers]]
driver = "desktop"
events = ["given_up"]
```

The script:

```python
import os
print(f"hello from {os.environ['AGENT_NAME']}")
# exit 0 = success; any non-zero = attempt_failed
```

Install:

```bash
dotagent doctor                # validate
dotagent run my-agent          # foreground smoke test
dotagent install               # write launchd plist / systemd unit
launchctl bootstrap "gui/$(id -u)" ~/Library/LaunchAgents/run.avelino.dotagent.plist
```

---

## 3. `agent.toml` ‚Äî every field

```toml
# ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ [agent] ‚Äî identity (required) ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
[agent]
name = "my-agent"                  # REQUIRED. Unique within dotagent root.
description = "What it does."      # optional
monitor = true                     # default true. false = excluded from `tick`
timeout_seconds = 1800             # default 1800. Hard kill (SIGTERM‚ÜíSIGKILL).

# ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ [run] ‚Äî how to invoke (required) ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
[run]
command = "fish"                   # REQUIRED. Executable.
args = ["./agent.fish"]            # static argv prefix
working_dir = "."                  # optional, relative to manifest dir

# ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ [env] ‚Äî environment injection (optional) ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
[env]
inherit = true                     # default true. false = hermetic env.
[env.extra]                        # merged on top
LOG_LEVEL = "info"
PYTHONUNBUFFERED = "1"

# ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ [defaults] ‚Äî agent-wide retry/backoff (optional) ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
[defaults]
max_retries = 3                    # 0 = no retry
retry_backoff_minutes = [5, 15, 30]
stale_after_minutes = 120          # window older than this ‚Üí state=stale

# ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ [[schedules]] ‚Äî REQUIRED, at least one ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
[[schedules]]
id = "daily"                       # REQUIRED. Unique within manifest.
type = "cron"                      # "cron" | "interval" | "expression"
weekdays = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]         # 0=Sun..6=Sat. cron only.
hours = [8]                        # cron only. Any of 0..23.
minute = 30                        # cron only. 0..59.
args = ["--period", "yesterday"]   # appended to [run].args
# Per-schedule overrides (override [defaults] above):
max_retries = 20
retry_backoff_minutes = [30]
stale_after_minutes = 240

[[schedules]]
id = "every-90"
type = "interval"
interval_minutes = 90              # interval only.

[[schedules]]
id = "linux-only"
type = "expression"
expression = "Mon..Fri *-*-* 08:00:00"  # systemd OnCalendar format (NOT cron syntax!)

# ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ [[preflight]] ‚Äî abort the run if any check fails (optional) ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
[[preflight]]
plugin = "preflight-warp"
config = { connect_command = "warp-cli connect" }

# ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ [[notifiers]] ‚Äî built-in, in-process (optional) ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
[[notifiers]]
driver = "imessage"                # built-in driver names below
to = "+5511999999999"
rate_limit_minutes = 60
events = ["given_up"]              # empty / absent = all events

# ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ [[on_success]] / [[on_failure]] ‚Äî plugin hooks (optional) ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
[[on_success]]
plugin = "sink-file"
config = { path = "/Users/me/reports/today.md", mode = "overwrite" }

# ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ [security] ‚Äî declared intent, schema-only in v0 (optional) ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
[security]
allowed_commands = ["python3", "/usr/bin/python3"]
allowed_plugins = ["preflight-warp", "sink-roam"]
network = "allow"                  # "allow" | "deny" | ["host1", "host2"]
filesystem_writable = ["/Users/me/reports"]
env_passthrough = ["PATH", "HOME", "LANG"]
```

### Field-by-field quick reference

| Block          | Field                  | Type            | Required | Default                |
|----------------|------------------------|-----------------|----------|------------------------|
| `[agent]`      | `name`                 | string          | yes      | ‚Äî                      |
| `[agent]`      | `description`          | string          | no       | ‚Äî                      |
| `[agent]`      | `monitor`              | bool            | no       | `true`                 |
| `[agent]`      | `timeout_seconds`      | int             | no       | `1800`                 |
| `[run]`        | `command`              | string          | yes      | ‚Äî                      |
| `[run]`        | `args`                 | `string[]`      | no       | `[]`                   |
| `[run]`        | `working_dir`          | string          | no       | manifest dir           |
| `[env]`        | `inherit`              | bool            | no       | `true`                 |
| `[env.extra]`  | (any key)              | string          | no       | ‚Äî                      |
| `[defaults]`   | `max_retries`          | int             | no       | `0`                    |
| `[defaults]`   | `retry_backoff_minutes`| `int[]`         | no       | `[]`                   |
| `[defaults]`   | `stale_after_minutes`  | int             | no       | (no stale check)       |
| `[[schedules]]`| `id`                   | string          | yes      | ‚Äî                      |
| `[[schedules]]`| `type`                 | `"cron"`/`"interval"`/`"expression"` | yes | ‚Äî      |
| `[[schedules]]`| `weekdays`             | `int[]` (0..6)  | cron     | (any)                  |
| `[[schedules]]`| `hours`                | `int[]` (0..23) | cron     | (any)                  |
| `[[schedules]]`| `minute`               | int (0..59)     | cron     | `0`                    |
| `[[schedules]]`| `interval_minutes`     | int             | interval | ‚Äî                      |
| `[[schedules]]`| `expression`           | string          | expr     | ‚Äî (systemd OnCalendar) |
| `[[schedules]]`| `args`                 | `string[]`      | no       | `[]`                   |
| `[[preflight]]`| `plugin`               | string          | yes      | ‚Äî                      |
| `[[preflight]]`| `config`               | table           | no       | `{}`                   |
| `[[notifiers]]`| `driver`               | string          | yes      | ‚Äî                      |
| `[[notifiers]]`| `events`               | `string[]`      | no       | (all events)           |
| `[[notifiers]]`| (driver-specific)      | varies          | varies   | varies                 |
| `[[on_success]]`/`[[on_failure]]` | `plugin`    | string | yes      | ‚Äî                      |
| `[[on_success]]`/`[[on_failure]]` | `config`    | table  | no       | `{}`                   |
| `[[on_success]]`/`[[on_failure]]` | `events`    | `string[]` | no  | (all events)           |

---

## 4. Environment variables dotagent injects

When the daemon spawns your agent, these `AGENT_*` vars are set on top
of the inherited parent env (unless `env.inherit = false`).

| Variable               | Type        | Example                                            |
|------------------------|-------------|----------------------------------------------------|
| `AGENT_NAME`           | string      | `finops-weekly`                                    |
| `AGENT_HOME`           | abs path    | `/Users/me/.config/dotagent/agents/finops-weekly`  |
| `AGENT_TMPDIR`         | abs path    | `/var/folders/.../tmpAbCdEf` (auto-cleaned on exit)|
| `AGENT_DRY_RUN`        | `"true"`/`"false"` | `"false"`                                   |
| `AGENT_SCHEDULE_ID`    | string      | `daily`                                            |
| `AGENT_SLUG`           | string      | `period_yesterday` (derived from schedule args)    |
| `AGENT_START_EPOCH`    | int         | `1700000000`                                       |
| `AGENT_ARGV`           | JSON array  | `["--period","yesterday"]`                         |
| `AGENT_HEARTBEAT_FILE` | abs path    | (empty when `AGENT_DRY_RUN=true`)                  |

> **Important**: `AGENT_HEARTBEAT_FILE` is **read-only from the agent's
> perspective**. dotagent writes the heartbeat before and after the run.
> Do NOT have your agent write to that path ‚Äî you'll race with the
> daemon. If you need to persist arbitrary state, use `AGENT_HOME` or
> a sink plugin.

### Slug derivation

`AGENT_SLUG` comes from the schedule's `args`:

| args                          | slug                  |
|-------------------------------|-----------------------|
| `[]`                          | `default`             |
| `["--period", "yesterday"]`   | `period_yesterday`    |
| `["--mode", "unsubscribe"]`   | `mode_unsubscribe`    |
| `["foo bar"]`                 | `foo_bar`             |

Rules: strip leading dashes, lowercase, replace non-alphanumeric with
`_`, collapse repeated `_`, trim trailing `_`. Empty ‚Üí `default`.

### Reading env vars

```fish
echo "$AGENT_NAME ran with schedule $AGENT_SCHEDULE_ID"
test "$AGENT_DRY_RUN" = "true"; and exit 0
cd $AGENT_TMPDIR
```

```python
import os, json
name = os.environ["AGENT_NAME"]
argv = json.loads(os.environ.get("AGENT_ARGV", "[]"))
if os.environ.get("AGENT_DRY_RUN") == "true":
    exit(0)
```

```go
name := os.Getenv("AGENT_NAME")
var argv []string
json.Unmarshal([]byte(os.Getenv("AGENT_ARGV")), &argv)
```

```bash
: "${AGENT_NAME:?running outside dotagent?}"
cd "$AGENT_TMPDIR" || exit
```

---

## 5. Schedules

Three types. dotagent translates each into the native OS scheduler.

### `type = "cron"`

Fires when `weekday matches AND hour matches AND minute matches`. Uses
launchd `StartCalendarInterval` (macOS) / systemd `OnCalendar` (Linux).

```toml
[[schedules]]
id = "daily-weekdays-8am"
type = "cron"
weekdays = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]   # Mon..Fri (0=Sun..6=Sat)
hours = [8]
minute = 30                  # daily at 08:30 Mon..Fri
```

```toml
[[schedules]]
id = "twice-a-day"
type = "cron"
hours = [9, 18]              # any weekday
minute = 0                   # daily at 09:00 and 18:00
```

### `type = "interval"`

Fires every `interval_minutes`. Uses launchd `StartInterval` (macOS) /
systemd `OnUnitActiveSec` (Linux).

```toml
[[schedules]]
id = "every-90-min"
type = "interval"
interval_minutes = 90
```

### `type = "expression"` (Linux only)

Free-form systemd `OnCalendar` syntax. **NOT cron syntax** ‚Äî this is
systemd's calendar event format.

```toml
[[schedules]]
id = "linux-weekdays-9am"
type = "expression"
expression = "Mon..Fri *-*-* 09:00:00"   # systemd OnCalendar
```

Quick `OnCalendar` examples:
- `*-*-* 08:00:00` ‚Äî daily at 08:00
- `Mon..Fri *-*-* 08:30:00` ‚Äî weekdays at 08:30
- `*-*-01 09:00:00` ‚Äî first day of each month at 09:00
- `*-*-* *:00:00` ‚Äî every hour on the hour
- `Mon *-*-* 09:00:00` ‚Äî every Monday at 09:00

If you want cross-platform, **use `cron` or `interval`**, not `expression`.

---

## 6. Notifier drivers (built-in, in-process)

Drivers live inside the daemon (crate `dotagent-notify`). No subprocess
fork, no extra binaries on `$PATH`.

| Driver     | Transport                                | Subprocess?            |
|------------|------------------------------------------|------------------------|
| `desktop`  | `NSUserNotification` / D-Bus             | No                     |
| `slack`    | HTTPS POST to Slack Incoming Webhooks    | No                     |
| `ntfy`     | HTTPS POST to ntfy.sh (or self-hosted)   | No                     |
| `pushover` | HTTPS POST to api.pushover.net           | No                     |
| `imessage` | `osascript` Messages.app automation      | **Yes** (no Apple API) |
| `plugin`   | Escape hatch ‚Üí plugin protocol           | Yes                    |

### `desktop`

```toml
[[notifiers]]
driver = "desktop"
title = "my-agent"          # default: agent name
subtitle = "free space low" # macOS only
sound = true                # macOS only
urgency = "critical"        # Linux only: low | normal | critical
icon = "dialog-warning"     # Linux only: icon name or absolute path
expire_ms = 5000            # Linux only: 0 = persistent
events = ["given_up"]
```

### `slack`

```toml
[[notifiers]]
driver = "slack"
webhook_url = "https://hooks.slack.com/services/T0/B0/XXX"
channel = "#alerts"         # optional
username = "dotagent"       # optional
icon_emoji = ":robot_face:" # optional
events = ["given_up", "recovered"]
```

### `ntfy`

```toml
[[notifiers]]
driver = "ntfy"
topic = "my-alerts"
base_url = "https://ntfy.sh"   # default; self-host friendly
token = "tk_..."               # optional bearer auth
priority = 4                   # 1..5
title = "disk-alert"           # default: agent name
tags = ["warning", "skull"]
events = ["given_up"]
```

### `pushover`

```toml
[[notifiers]]
driver = "pushover"
token = "<application-token>"
user  = "<user-key>"
priority = 1
title = "disk-alert"           # default: agent name
events = ["given_up"]
```

### `imessage` (macOS only)

```toml
[[notifiers]]
driver = "imessage"
to = "+5511999999999"            # phone or email iMessage handle
rate_limit_minutes = 60          # skip-if-recent; 0 disables
events = ["given_up"]
```

### `plugin` (escape hatch for third-party notifiers)

```toml
[[notifiers]]
driver = "plugin"
name = "notify-discord"          # plugin binary: dotagent-plugin-notify-discord
events = ["given_up"]
[notifiers.config]
webhook_url = "https://discord.com/api/webhooks/..."
```

---

## 7. Built-in plugins (preflight + sink)

Plugins are external binaries (`dotagent-plugin-<name>`) speaking JSON
over stdio. The dotagent install (via Homebrew or release tarball)
ships these four first-party plugins.

### `preflight-warp` ‚Äî abort if Cloudflare WARP is disconnected

```toml
[[preflight]]
plugin = "preflight-warp"
config = { connect_command = "warp-cli connect" }  # optional hint shown in failure notification
# Optional: { binary = "/usr/local/bin/warp-cli" }
```

### `preflight-cmd` ‚Äî abort if a command fails

```toml
[[preflight]]
plugin = "preflight-cmd"
config = {
  command = "gh",
  args = ["auth", "status"],
  expect_exit = 0,                # default 0
  expect_contains = "Logged in"   # optional substring in stdout/stderr
}
```

### `sink-file` ‚Äî write agent stdout to a file

```toml
[[on_success]]
plugin = "sink-file"
config = { path = "/Users/me/reports/today.md", mode = "overwrite" }
# mode: "overwrite" (default) | "append"
```

### `sink-roam` ‚Äî publish agent stdout to a Roam page (via `mcp` CLI)

```toml
[[on_success]]
plugin = "sink-roam"
config = {
  page = "today",                        # or "April 22nd, 2026" or "acme/tech/aws/X"
  marker_regex = "#DORA.*yesterday",     # idempotent replace (deletes match, re-creates)
  mcp_binary = "~/.cargo/bin/mcp"        # default
}
```

Roam-specific: `page` accepts `today` (resolves to current daily note),
a named daily in **ordinal format** (`April 22nd, 2026` ‚Äî not ISO!), or
a namespaced page path (`acme/tech/aws/finops-2026`, created if missing).

Stdin expected format (parsed as hierarchy):

```
#TAG First line is ROOT (no indentation)
  L1 header ‚Äî direct child (indent ‚â§ 3 spaces)
    L2 child of previous L1 (indent > 3 spaces)
  Another L1
    L2 of that L1
---
Notes after `---` separator are cut.
```

---

## 8. Events

| Event           | Fires when                                          |
|-----------------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| `attempt_failed`| Run exited non-zero (a retry may still happen)      |
| `timed_out`     | Run exceeded `agent.timeout_seconds`                |
| `given_up`      | All retries exhausted ‚Äî operator action expected     |
| `recovered`     | A previously-failing schedule passed                 |
| `success`       | Every successful run (use sparingly ‚Äî noisy)         |
| `preflight`     | A preflight plugin returned `ok: false`              |
| `daily_summary` | Daemon's internal 22:45 health roll-up               |

`[[notifiers]]` and `[[on_*]]` accept `events = [...]` to filter. Empty
or absent = all events.

---

## 9. Exit code semantics

dotagent only cares about **zero vs non-zero**.

| Exit code      | Meaning                              | Event fired       |
|----------------|--------------------------------------|-------------------|
| `0`            | Success                              | `success` (+ `recovered` if previously failing) |
| any non-zero   | Failure attempt                      | `attempt_failed`  |
| (SIGKILL by daemon) | Exceeded `timeout_seconds`      | `timed_out`       |

There's no special handling for `2`, `127`, etc. If you want to surface
the *kind* of failure to a sink/notifier, write a structured line to
stderr ‚Äî dotagent captures the last 5 stderr lines into the failure
message.

---

## 10. Heartbeat shape

dotagent writes a heartbeat file before AND after every non-dry-run
execution. Your agent should treat this as **read-only**:

```
~/.config/dotagent/state/agents/{name}/{slug}.heartbeat.json
```

Shape (read this if you want to query history from another agent):

```jsonc
{
  "name": "my-agent",
  "slug": "default",
  "args": [],
  "started_at": 1700000000,
  "started_at_iso": "2023-11-14T22:13:20+0000",
  "finished_at": 1700000100,
  "finished_at_iso": "2023-11-14T22:15:00+0000",
  "exit_code": 0,
  "duration_seconds": 100,
  "last_success_at": 1700000100,         // preserved; never overwritten on failure
  "last_success_at_iso": "2023-11-14T22:15:00+0000"
}
```

---

## 11. Health states

Computed per `(agent, schedule)` pair on every `tick`:

| State     | Meaning                                                                       |
|-----------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `ok`      | `last_success_at >= expected_at` and no retries needed                        |
| `degraded`| Recovered after `attempts > 0`, OR interval-style overdue but within `2*interval` |
| `failing` | Window passed without success; retrying or already given up                   |
| `stale`   | Never ran, OR window older than `stale_after_minutes`                         |

---

## 12. Common `dotagent doctor` errors

| Error message                                  | Cause                                                    |
|-----------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
| `agent name missing`                           | `[agent].name` not set                                   |
| `agent name not unique`                        | Two manifests claim the same `name`                      |
| `at least one [[schedules]] required`          | Manifest has no schedules                                |
| `schedule id missing`                          | `[[schedules]].id` not set                               |
| `schedule id not unique within manifest`       | Two schedules share the same `id`                        |
| `weekdays out of range`                        | A value in `weekdays` is not in `0..=6`                  |
| `hours out of range`                           | A value in `hours` is not in `0..=23`                    |
| `minute out of range`                          | `minute` not in `0..=59`                                 |
| `interval_minutes required for type=interval`  | Missing field on interval schedule                       |
| `expression required for type=expression`      | Missing field on expression schedule                     |
| `expression type only supported on Linux`      | Used `expression` on macOS                               |
| `command missing`                              | `[run].command` not set                                  |
| `unknown notifier driver`                      | `[[notifiers]].driver` is not one of the 6 valid values  |
| `notifier slack: webhook_url required`         | (driver-specific required field)                         |
| `plugin <name> not found on $PATH`             | The plugin binary isn't installed                        |
| `‚öÝ no [security] section`                      | Harmless warning; v0 is schema-only                      |

---

## 13. Complete examples (combining features)

### Example A ‚Äî Python agent, cron weekday, Slack notifier, preflight + sink

```toml
# ~/.config/dotagent/agents/team-standup/agent.toml
[agent]
name = "team-standup"
description = "Daily team summary from GitHub + Sentry, posted to Slack and Roam."
timeout_seconds = 600

[run]
command = "uv"
args = ["run", "./agent.py"]

[env.extra]
LOG_LEVEL = "info"

[defaults]
max_retries = 5
retry_backoff_minutes = [10, 20, 40, 80, 160]
stale_after_minutes = 240

[[schedules]]
id = "daily"
type = "cron"
weekdays = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]   # Mon..Fri
hours = [8]
minute = 30

# Don't run if VPN is down ‚Äî retries will keep trying every backoff.
[[preflight]]
plugin = "preflight-warp"
config = { connect_command = "warp-cli connect" }

# Tier 1: noisy desktop banner on every failed attempt
[[notifiers]]
driver = "desktop"
title = "team-standup"
events = ["attempt_failed", "given_up"]

# Tier 2: Slack only after retries exhausted
[[notifiers]]
driver = "slack"
webhook_url = "https://hooks.slack.com/services/T0/B0/XXX"
channel = "#eng-ops"
events = ["given_up", "recovered"]

# Persist the rendered output to today's Roam daily note
[[on_success]]
plugin = "sink-roam"
config = { page = "today", marker_regex = "#STANDUP.*\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}" }

[security]
allowed_commands = ["uv", "python3"]
allowed_plugins  = ["preflight-warp", "sink-roam"]
network          = ["api.github.com", "acme.sentry.io", "hooks.slack.com"]
filesystem_writable = []
env_passthrough  = ["PATH", "HOME", "GITHUB_TOKEN", "SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN"]
```

### Example B ‚Äî Go binary, interval, iMessage on give-up

```toml
# ~/.config/dotagent/agents/disk-alert/agent.toml
[agent]
name = "disk-alert"
description = "Alert when free space on / drops below threshold."
timeout_seconds = 30

[run]
command = "./agent"          # compiled Go binary in the agent dir

[env.extra]
DISK_FREE_MIN_PCT = "20"
DISK_CHECK_MOUNT = "/"

[defaults]
max_retries = 3
retry_backoff_minutes = [5, 15, 30]
stale_after_minutes = 120

[[schedules]]
id = "every-15min"
type = "interval"
interval_minutes = 15

[[notifiers]]
driver = "desktop"
title = "disk-alert"
subtitle = "free space low"
sound = true
urgency = "critical"
events = ["attempt_failed", "given_up"]

[[notifiers]]
driver = "imessage"
to = "+5511999999999"
rate_limit_minutes = 60        # at most 1 ping/hour even on extended failure
events = ["given_up"]
```

### Example C ‚Äî Pure shell collector, daily, file sink only

```toml
# ~/.config/dotagent/agents/hn-morning/agent.toml
[agent]
name = "hn-morning"
description = "Top 5 HN stories at 08:00 weekdays."
timeout_seconds = 60

[run]
command = "fish"
args = ["./agent.fish"]

[[schedules]]
id = "weekdays-8am"
type = "cron"
weekdays = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
hours = [8]
minute = 0

[[on_success]]
plugin = "sink-file"
config = { path = "/Users/me/reports/hn-today.md", mode = "overwrite" }
```

---

## 14. What dotagent does NOT do

- **Run your business logic.** It's a scheduler + supervisor.
- **Provide an SDK.** Read env vars, write stdout/stderr, exit. That's
  the entire API surface.
- **Sleep waiting for time.** The OS scheduler fires the trigger.
- **Substitute the `mcp` CLI.** `sink-roam` calls `mcp` separately.
- **Run on Windows.** Linux + WSL works; native Windows doesn't.
- **Template `path` or any config field.** No `{{vars}}` ‚Äî your agent
  must build dynamic paths itself.

---

## 15. Filesystem layout

```
~/.config/dotagent/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ agents/<name>/agent.toml         # manifests
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ state/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ agents/<name>/<slug>.heartbeat.json
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ windows/<name>-<slug>-<ts>.json
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ known_manifests.json
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ daemon.pid
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ logs/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ daemon/dotagent.log          # daemon stdio + tracing
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ agents/<name>/<name>.log     # rotated; per-agent
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ audit.log                        # JSONL audit trail
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ config.toml                      # optional dotagent config
```

Discovery order for manifests:

1. Every directory in `$DOTAGENT_ROOT` (colon-separated)
2. `$DOTAGENT_HOME/agents/` (default `~/.config/dotagent/agents/`)
3. `$CWD/agents/`
4. `$CWD`

Discovery order for plugin binaries:

1. Every directory in `$DOTAGENT_PLUGIN_PATH`
2. `$DOTAGENT_HOME/plugins/`
3. `/usr/local/lib/dotagent/plugins/`
4. `$PATH`

---

## 16. Env vars dotagent reads (overrides)

| Variable                       | Purpose                                                              | Default              |
|--------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------|
| `DOTAGENT_HOME`                | Root directory                                                       | `~/.config/dotagent` |
| `DOTAGENT_ROOT`                | Extra manifest search dirs (colon-separated), prepended to default   | (empty)              |
| `DOTAGENT_PLUGIN_PATH`         | Extra plugin search dirs (colon-separated), prepended to default     | (empty)              |
| `RUST_LOG`                     | Tracing filter; overrides `[logging].level` in `config.toml`         | `info`               |
| `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS`   | OTLP auth headers (comma-separated `k=v`); used when OTLP enabled    | (empty)              |

---

## 17. CLI surface (most common)

```bash
dotagent doctor                  # validate every manifest discoverable
dotagent run <name>              # foreground run for smoke-testing
dotagent run <name> --schedule <id> --dry-run
dotagent status                  # health dashboard
dotagent logs <name> --follow    # tail an agent's log
dotagent tick                    # what would the daemon do right now?
dotagent reload                  # SIGHUP daemon ‚Äî picks up manifest changes
dotagent install                 # write launchd plist / systemd unit
dotagent uninstall               # remove launchd plist / systemd unit
dotagent plugin list             # list resolved plugin binaries
dotagent inspect <name>          # heartbeat + windows + schedule resolution
dotagent daily-summary           # ad-hoc daily roll-up
```

---

## 18. Patterns (pick the closest shape)

| Pattern                | Shape                                                                | When to use                              |
|------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------|
| Pure data collector    | APIs ‚Üí jq/filter ‚Üí sink                                              | Output is structured data, no LLM needed |
| Brief / digest         | Sources ‚Üí LLM (cheap model) ‚Üí notifier                               | Input is noisy, want 3 bullets           |
| Triage / classifier    | Source ‚Üí heuristic for 80% ‚Üí LLM for residue ‚Üí apply labels          | Mostly deterministic, LLM for the tail   |
| Generator + idempotent | Sources ‚Üí LLM ‚Üí sink with `marker_regex` (replace, don't append)     | Re-runs shouldn't duplicate              |
| Watchdog / preflight   | `preflight-cmd` or `preflight-warp` ‚Üí empty agent body ‚Üí notifier    | Just want to alert on a condition        |

---

## 19. Deep dives (when this digest isn't enough)

- Full schema: `docs/reference/agent-spec.md`
- Env vars (every detail): `docs/reference/env-vars.md`
- Plugin protocol (JSON shape, exit codes, env): `docs/reference/plugin-protocol.md`
- Notifications (driver internals): `docs/concepts/notifications.md`
- Plugins (creating your own): `docs/concepts/plugins.md`
- Patterns + extending agents: `docs/concepts/agents.md`
- Architecture: `docs/concepts/architecture.md`
- CLI reference (every flag): `docs/reference/cli.md`
- Filesystem layout (every path): `docs/reference/paths.md`
- Daemon lifecycle (install/start/stop/reload): `docs/guides/daemon-lifecycle.md`
- Troubleshooting (sintoma ‚Üí cause): `docs/guides/troubleshooting.md`
- Observability (logs, OTel, vendor recipes): `docs/guides/observability.md`
- Config reference (`config.toml`): `docs/guides/config-reference.md`
- Threat model: `docs/security/threat-model.md`
- Migrating from Fish framework: `docs/guides/migrating-from-fish.md`
- Per-plugin docs: `docs/plugins/<name>.md`
- Worked examples: `examples/hello-fish/`, `examples/hello-python/`,
  `examples/hello-go/`, `examples/hello-rust/`, `examples/disk-alert/`

---

## 20. Sanity check before reporting "done"

When you, the LLM, generate an `agent.toml`, verify against this digest:

1. [ ] `[agent].name` is unique and matches the directory name (advisory)
2. [ ] `[run].command` resolves on `$PATH` (or is absolute)
3. [ ] At least one `[[schedules]]` with a unique `id`
4. [ ] Schedule fields match the `type`: cron needs `weekdays/hours/minute`,
   interval needs `interval_minutes`, expression needs `expression`
5. [ ] `weekdays` in `0..=6`, `hours` in `0..=23`, `minute` in `0..=59`
6. [ ] If `type = "expression"`, the syntax is systemd `OnCalendar`
   (NOT cron), and the agent is documented as Linux-only
7. [ ] Each `[[notifiers]].driver` is one of: `desktop`, `slack`, `ntfy`,
   `pushover`, `imessage`, `plugin`
8. [ ] Driver-specific required fields are present (e.g. `slack` needs
   `webhook_url`, `imessage` needs `to`, `pushover` needs `token`+`user`)
9. [ ] Plugin names in `[[preflight]]` / `[[on_*]]` exist in
   `docs/plugins/` (or are third-party plugins the user installed)
10. [ ] The agent script reads env vars (`AGENT_*`) ‚Äî does NOT import an SDK
11. [ ] The agent script does NOT write to `AGENT_HEARTBEAT_FILE`
12. [ ] If you used a `[security]` block, the `network` entries match
   what the agent script actually contacts
